name: The Sanctioned Thief
concept: You steal with a license. The Thieves' Guild takes its cut and, in return, no one is supposed to slit your throat for working their city. It is a good arrangement, so long as you never forget who truly owns your hands.
duty: The Guild grants the right to steal and can revoke it with a word. Unsanctioned, you are just meat the city has not finished with yet.

look.label: Guild mark
options: a tattoo where clothes hide it
a scar the Guild gave as warning

gear.label: Tools
options: a roll of picks and probes

gear.label: Edge
options: a map of the quarter's rooftops
a bag of caltrops and smoke
keys that fit more locks than they should

moves.name: The Quiet Job
moves.trigger: When you break in, lift a prize, and get out without raising the house.

results.success: You get the prize and get clear.
results.failure: You get one but not the other — caught with the goods, or clear but empty-handed.
advantage: Hold 1 Score (spend: you also grabbed something extra and valuable / you left no trace at all / you know a second way out / you spotted the trap before it sprung).
You learn the house's real secret — who they fear, what else they hide.
threat: You leave a thread that leads back to the Black Cat.
Add 1 Heat: a witness, an alarm, or a name spoken.
results.triumph: A perfect job — they won't even know they were robbed until it's far too late.
results.despair: The job was bait, and the jaws are closing now.

hold.name: Score
spend: You grabbed something extra and valuable on the way.
You left no trace; no one knows a theft occurred.
You have a second, unwatched way out.
You spotted and beat a trap or guard before it mattered.

moves.name: Sanctioned
moves.trigger: When you lean on your Guild standing for a fence, a safe rooftop, a name, or a blind eye (Invoke Patron, core.md):
moves.text: The Guild provides, and adds 1 Debt. Steal without sanction, cheat the Guild of its cut, or embarrass it, and you risk being declared unsanctioned — after which every knife in the city is licensed against you.

moves.name: Cat-Foot
moves.trigger: When you climb, balance, tumble, or move without a sound.

results.success: You go where you please, quiet and unseen.
results.failure: You slip, or you're spotted at the worst moment.
advantage: Bring another along, just as quiet.
You end up perfectly placed for what's next.
threat: You make it, but leave a mark or a sound behind you.
You're committed; there's no going back the way you came.
results.triumph: You move like the fog itself; no one will believe you were there.
results.despair: You're seen by exactly the wrong pair of eyes.

moves.name: Sleight
moves.trigger: When you pick a pocket or a lock, palm a thing, or plant one on someone.

results.failure: A hand closes on your wrist, or the lock defeats you noisily.
advantage: Grab a little extra while you're at it.
They'll never suspect it was you.
threat: You leave a trace that points back.
It takes longer than you have.
results.triumph: You do it under their nose and they smile at you.
results.despair: You're caught red-handed by someone who matters.

moves.name: Case the Mark
moves.trigger: When you study a place or a person you mean to rob.

results.success: Ask the GM one: where's the prize? what guards it? when is it weakest?
results.failure: You misjudge it, and the mistake waits for you inside.
advantage: You find a way in no one else would think of.
threat: Someone noticed you casing the place.
Your read is already going stale.
results.triumph: You know the job cold, every guard and every gap.
results.despair: While you cased them, they cased you.

moves.name: Guild Ties
moves.trigger: When you need a fence, a forger, a lookout, or a name in a hurry:
moves.text: Name a Guild contact you can reach here and take a boost die dealing with them. The GM says what small price or gossip they attach to the favor.

bonds: ____ has fenced for me since we were both children in the gutter.
____ took the fall for a job that was mine, and I still owe them for it.
The Guild told me to watch ____, and I have been lying about what I see.
____ thinks I can get us out of the cellar with one good score, and I've promised it.
I stole something from ____ once, and they've never known it was me.
communityTie: The Black Cat eats when your jobs pay; ____ frets every time the Guild's cut comes due.
The Guild would love a set of eyes inside the band; ____ is the one you'll never sell.
Old Sabra keeps the den because your scores keep the rent paid; you both pretend not to know it.

tracks.name: Debt
tracks.note: At 6 the Guild makes a major demand, then resets to 3.

tracks.name: Heat
tracks.note: Attention from the watch and your marks (core.md).

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Score by 1.
Learn the rooftops and drains of a quarter so well you can never be truly cornered there.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Buy down your Guild Debt by 1, permanently, with a fat cut.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Go unsanctioned on your own terms: cut ties with the Guild, resolving the fallout in play.
Rise in the Guild: become a master with a crew and a Bastion (a safehouse) of your own.
